entry.text: A sound like the sky tearing at the seams. Then a double sonic boom that vibrates your back teeth. A trail of black smoke arcs overhead in a parabola that ends approximately twenty metres in front of you with a concussive thud and a spray of displaced earth. The impact crater is small — whatever fell was dense, not large. The smoke is not fire-smoke. You approach cautiously. Something sits in the centre of the crater, still ticking with heat: a black box covered in symbols you don't recognise. It fits in a hand. It is warm. It is not damaged.

choices.label: Pick it up and pocket it immediately
Nudge it with a stick first, then pick it up

device_retrieved.text: It sits in your pack, ticking quietly. It smells faintly of ozone and something else you can't place — something almost floral. The symbols on its surface shift slightly when you're not looking directly at it. You are probably overthinking this. You are probably not.

device_shock.text: The moment you touch it, a brief pulse of energy fires up your arm. Not enough to do real damage — more like a warning. Your fingers tingle for the next hour. The device sits in your pack perfectly still.
